Project.name: Bayesian_Lunar_Lander_Gymnasium
Project.Overview: An autonomous agent for Gymnasium's LunarLander-v3. Bypasses Deep RL (PPO/DQN) to implement a Physics-First Bayesian control law. We treat the environment's 6 continuous observation fields as noisy sensors and maintain a posterior belief via a Kalman Filter. The agent picks actions using Bayesian Decision Theory (maximising Expected Utility).

Environment.API: import gymnasium as gym env = gym.make("LunarLander-v3", render_mode="human") obs, info = env.reset(seed=42) obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info = env.step(action) env.close()
Environment.Constraint: Treat the first 6 elements of obs as noisy observations Z_t for the Kalman Filter. Do not use obs directly for control; use the filter's posterior mean (mu).

Layer.name: KalmanFilter
Layer.Purpose: Maintain a Gaussian posterior N(mu, P) over the 6-D continuous state [x, y, vx, vy, angle, ang_vel].
Layer.Implementation: Prediction: x_pred = A x + b(action) (Newtonian kinematics) Update: x_post = x_pred + K (z - H x_pred) (Bayesian update) All matrix math via NumPy only (no scipy, no filterpy).

Layer.name: HeuristicPolicy
Layer.UtilityFunction:
U(a) = -10 |vy| - 5 |vx| - 10 |angle| - 5 |x| - fuel(a)
fuel(a): a=0 -> 0.00 (no-op) a=1 -> -0.03 (side thruster) a=2 -> -0.30 (main engine) a=3 -> -0.03 (side thruster)
Evaluated on the PREDICTED next-state from the Kalman filter.
Layer.DescentRatePriority: Hard override: if current vy < -0.2 (descending too fast), fire main engine (action 2) immediately before evaluating utility. This doubles down on the high w_vy penalty and ensures the bot never free-falls into the pad.
Layer.PadTargeting: The -5|x| term ensures the agent is always penalised for drifting away from the pad centre (x=0), preventing off-screen drift.
Layer.DecisionRule: a* = argmax_a U(a) (unless vy override fires)

Layer.name: SafetyModel
Layer.CrashBiasedPrior:
P(Safe) is computed via a sigmoid over a "danger" metric derived from the Kalman state:
danger = 10|vy| + 5|vx| + 10|angle| + 5|x| P(Safe) = sigma(danger / 8.0 - 0.5)
The low shift (0.5) acts as a Laplace-style crash-biased prior, equivalent to Beta(alpha_crash=3, beta_safe=1). This means:
- At danger=0 (perfect state): P(Safe) ~ 0.62 (not 0.95) - At danger=4 (modest flight): P(Safe) ~ 0.50 - At danger=16 (rough state): P(Safe) ~ 0.18
The agent is never overconfident in safety, which lowers both ECE and Brier score compared to a neutral prior.

Layer.name: ValidationSuite
Layer.Component: Reliability diagram, Brier score, Expected Calibration Error.
Precision-Recall curve for Safe vs Crash classification.
Pearson correlation between predicted utility and realised return.
Fit main-engine frame counts to NegBin via method-of-moments.
Explicit PMF and Knuth sampler for offline event-rate analysis.

Project.TrainingLoop:
env = GymLunarLander() agent = BayesianLanderAgent()
for episode in range(500): obs, info = env.reset() agent.reset(obs)
while not done: p_crash = 1.0 - agent.safety_score() action = agent.select_action() # vy override or argmax U result = env.step(action) # step the REAL Gym env agent.observe(action, result.obs) # Kalman predict + update
log(crashed, fuel_counts, p_crash_per_step, episode_return)
# End: save calibration.png, precision_recall.png, NegBin fit.

File.name: config.py
File.desc: All hyperparameters: Kalman noise, utility weights (10/5/10/5/0.3), vy threshold, safety sigmoid params.

File.name: env_wrapper.py
File.desc: Thin wrapper around gym.make('LunarLander-v3'). No custom physics.

File.name: kalman_filter.py
File.desc: 6-D Kalman filter with Newtonian dynamics. Pure NumPy.

File.name: bayes_agent.py
File.desc: Agent: Kalman + U(a) = -10|vy| -5|vx| -10|ang| -5|x| -fuel. Early vy fire. Crash-biased P(Safe).

File.name: reward_model.py
File.desc: Reward decomposition into fuel / landing / crash components.

File.name: poisson_utils.py
File.desc: Poisson PMF and Knuth sampler (offline analysis only).

File.name: metrics.py
File.desc: Calibration, Brier, ECE, Precision/Recall, EV-vs-return. All from scratch.

File.name: train.py
File.desc: Main loop: run episodes, log data, print metrics, save plots.

Project.Constraint: NO PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-Learn. All Bayesian updates, matrix math, and statistical models are implemented from scratch with NumPy and math.
